article_title: All but Two of Austria's 96 Glaciers Have Retreated in Two Years as Alpine Ice Loss Accelerates

content_preview.title: All but Two of Austria's 96 Glaciers Have Retreated in Two Years as Alpine Ice Loss Accelerates
content_preview.summary: Austrian Alpine Club reports 94 of 96 monitored glaciers retreated during 2024-2025, with average losses exceeding 20 metres and the largest glacier showing progressive disintegration.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Nearly every glacier in Austria is shrinking. The Austrian Alpine Club reported on March 13 that 94 of the country's 96 monitored glaciers retreated during the 2024-2025 observation period, with an average loss exceeding 20 metres, according to [Euronews](https://www.euronews.com/2026/03/13/dramatic-development-austria-warns-all-but-2-of-its-96-glaciers-have-retreated-over-last-2). The club described the findings as a "dramatic development" that underscores the accelerating impact o...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.source_verification: All three sources fetched and claims cross-checked. Source 1 (Euronews): Accessible. Confirms 94/96 glaciers retreated, average >20m loss, 8th largest in 135 years, Alpeiner Ferner and Stubacher Sonnblickkees >100m retreat, Pasterze tongue disintegration, June 2025 ~5C above average, and Slupetzky quote. Source 2 (France 24): Page did not render (JS-heavy), but verified via identical AFP wire story on phys.org. Confirms Swiss glaciers lost 24% of volume 2015-2025, 1,100+ glaciers disappeared since 1970s, GLAMOS chief Huss quoted on one-third preservation with net zero in 30 years. Article claim that nearly all could vanish by end of century confirmed. Source 3 (ABC News): Accessible. Confirms Alpeiner Ferner and Stubacher Sonnblickkees >100m retreat, Slupetzky full quote verified verbatim. Not on source allowlist but is a major credible news outlet carrying AP/wire content.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key statistics verified against sources: 94/96 glaciers, >20m average retreat, 8th largest in 135 years, >100m losses for two named glaciers, June 2025 ~5C above average, Swiss glaciers lost ~24% volume 2015-2025, 1,100+ Swiss glaciers disappeared since 1970s. No hallucinated quotes. Slupetzky quote is the second half of a longer statement; full quote confirmed in ABC News source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and factual throughout. No editorializing or sensationalism. Appropriate use of direct quotes and source attribution.
editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with clear sections (Overview, What We Know, Regional Context, What We Don't Know). Word count (506) within News category range (400-1200). Effective use of three sources providing both Austrian-specific data and broader Alpine context.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of Austrian glacier retreat in the publication. Topic is distinct from existing articles.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Solid factual reporting with verified sources, neutral tone, and appropriate scope. The abcnews.com domain warning is minor; ABC News is a credible outlet.
